title: Aeschynanthus sikkimensis
taxonomy: Aeschynanthus sikkimensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The plant is commonly known for its vibrant flowers and is native to the Sikkim Himalayas. The classification of Aeschynanthus sikkimensis places it within the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants such as mint and snapdragons. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its leaf structure and flower morphology. The Gesneriaceae family, to which Aeschynanthus sikkimensis belongs, is a diverse group of flowering plants found primarily in tropical regions.

intro: Aeschynanthus sikkimensis is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the Gesneriaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aeschynanthus sikkimensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, and its preparations include herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as dried extracts and capsules.
